MEMO IN OPPOS. TO DEFT'S MTN TO SET ASIDE ORDER FILED BY PLAINTIFF TELEKENEX, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GEORGE GEORGIOU'S MOTION FOR ORDER SETTING ASIDE ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT ESTABLISHED A SUFFICIENT BASIS FOR SETTING ASIDE THE SEPTEMBER 6, 2013 ORDER BASED ON HIS HAVING FAILED TO FILE A REPLY BRIEF OR APPEAR AT THE HEARING BECAUSE HE MADE AN ERROR CONCERNING THE CALENDARING OF THE MOTION HE HAD FILED. THE PROVISION FOR MANDATORY RELIEF DUE TO ATTORNEY MISTAKE UNDER CCP 473(B) ONLY APPLIES TO RELIEF FROM A JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL OR DEFAULT. (GARCIA V. HEJMADI (1997) 58 CAL.APP.4TH 674, 681.) DEFENDANT IS SEEKING TO SET ASIDE AN ORDER DENYING HIS MOTION TO VACATE THE DEFAULT, NOT A DEFAULT JUDGMENT. IN THE PROPOSED DECLARATION OF GEORGE GEORGIOU, DEFENDANT STATES THAT HE CHOSE NOT TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT. ( 2-3.) THE PROVISION FOR DISCRETIONARY RELIEF UNDER CCP 473(B) REQUIRES THAT A PARTY SEEKING RELIEF "DEMONSTRATE THAT SUCH MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, OR GENERAL NEGLECT WAS EXCUSABLE." (ZAMORA V. CLAYBORN CONTRACTING GROUP, INC. (2002) 28 CAL.4TH 249, 258.) DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT A REASONABLY PRUDENT PERSON WOULD NOT HAVE CHECKED THE TENTATIVE RULINGS OR APPEARED AT A HEARING ON A DATE THAT HE SET. FURTHER, "CONDUCT FALLING BELOW THE PROFESSIONAL STANDARD OF CARE, SUCH AS FAILURE TO TIMELY OBJECT OR TO PROPERLY ADVANCE AN ARGUMENT, IS NOT THEREFORE EXCUSABLE." (ID.) THERE IS NO STATUTORY BASIS FOR SETTING ASIDE A DEFAULT BECAUSE A PARTY CHOSE NOT TO RESPOND TO A COMPLAINT, LET ALONE FOR SIMPLY FAILING TO FILE A REPLY BRIEF OR APPEAR AT A HEARING. THERE IS NO REASON TO THINK THAT IF A NEW HEARING WERE GRANTED THE RESULT WOULD BE ANY DIFFERENT GIVEN THE BASIS FOR THE MOTION IN THE FIRST PLACE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-11-2014 Continued To Jun-24-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-15-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn For Order Setting Aside Order Denying Deft'S Mtn To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Nov-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARK MEYER, ESQ., 415.956.8400, FOR PLAINTIFF TELEKENEX, INC.,; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GEORGE GEORGIOU'S Motion For Order Setting Aside Order Denying Defendant's Motion To Set Aside Default IS Denied. Defendant has not established a sufficient basis for setting aside the September 6, 2013 Order based on his having failed to file a reply brief or appear at the hearing because he made an error concerning the calendaring of the motion he had filed. The provision for mandatory relief due to attorney mistake under CCP 473(b) only applies to relief from a judgment of dismissal or default. (Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 681.) Defendant is seeking to set aside an order denying his motion to vacate the default, not a default judgment. In the Proposed Declaration of George Georgiou, Defendant states that he chose not to respond to the Complaint. ( 2-3.) The provision for discretionary relief under CCP 473(b) requires that a party seeking relief "demonstrate that such mistake, inadvertence, or general neglect was excusable." (Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 258.) Defendant has not demonstrated that a reasonably prudent person would not have checked the tentative rulings or appeared at a hearing on a date that he set. Further, "conduct falling below the professional standard of care, such as failure to timely object or to properly advance an argument, is not therefore excusable." (Id.) There is no statutory basis for setting aside a default because a party chose not to respond to a complaint, let alone for simply failing to file a reply brief or appear at a hearing. There is no reason to think that if a new hearing were granted the result would be any different given the basis for the motion in the first place. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
